question: For the `StringWrapper` class (from Q20, managing dynamic C-style string), implement the copy assignment operator (`operator=`). Ensure it handles self-assignment correctly and performs a deep copy of the managed C-style string to prevent resource leaks and double-free issues.

test_cases.input: StringWrapper s1("Hello"); StringWrapper s2("World"); s1 = s2;
StringWrapper s4("C++"); StringWrapper s3; s4 = s3;
StringWrapper s3; StringWrapper s5("Programming"); s3 = s5;
StringWrapper s1("Hello"); StringWrapper s3("Programming"); StringWrapper s4; s1 = s3 = s4;

errors: The copy assignment operator correctly handles deep copying and self-assignment, but it uses verbose manual resource management (explicit `delete[]` then `new char[]` and `strcpy`). This approach is less readable, more prone to errors, and less efficient than the copy-and-swap idiom, which delegates resource management to the copy constructor and destructor.
